title: Minister of Industry, Science, Technology and Innovation (MISTI)  |  Chairman, Khmer Enterprise Board of Trustees

leader.name: Hem Vanndy
leader.position: Minister of Industry, Science, Technology and Innovation (MISTI)
leader.purpose: Mental model schema mapping decision-making architecture across 7 dimensions New generation technocrat minister. Focus on Industry 4.0, SME formalisation, economic diversification, and industrial value-add. Chairs Khmer Enterprise (national entrepreneurship fund). Has pitched Cambodia to Singaporean investors and visited ADB HQ. Framing is entrepreneurship-enabling rather than state-led industrial policy.

growth_theory.assessed_position: Vanndy's growth model centres on the domestic productive base rather than FDI attraction (that's Chanthol's domain). His theory: Cambodia's 500,000+ MSMEs are a latent growth engine that requires formalisation, data visibility, and technology access to realise their potential. The 2023–2028 Strategic Plan on Informal Economic Development — which he co-chairs with the Minister of Labour — is the pr
growth_theory.primary_quote: You can't shake a tree that has just begun to grow. Instead, it's essential to nurture it with water, provide it with fertilisers and take care of it regularly. MISTI advocates for support, not interference in fostering private sector engagement.
growth_theory.primary_source: Hem Vanndy, DISTI visit, Prey Veng, 2024
subfields: Core Thesis: Industrial diversification through SME formalisation + Industry 4.0 adaptation

risk_tolerance.assessed_position: Vanndy pushed through the coordinated joint inter-ministerial inspection mechanism — replacing multiple separate ministry inspections with a single coordinated process — which directly attacked a source of rent-seeking by inspectors. This is a genuine governance risk within the system. His 2025 data synchronisation initiative requires ministries to share data and accept a common SME definition — a
subfields: Assessment: MEDIUM — Reform-minded but within political constraints

time_horizon.assessed_position: Vanndy's 2025 planning conference projections (5.2% GDP in 2025, 5% in 2026) are based on Industrial sector growth of 7.2% — realistic near-term targets. His LDC graduation references are consistent (every speech mentions 2029 as the planning horizon) but his implementation tools are 5-year strategy documents, not 25-year industrial roadmaps. He is working to the right timeline but needs to accele
subfields: Revealed: 5 years (Pentagonal Strategy Phase I)  |  Aspirational: 2030

dependency_assumptions.assessed_position: 1. Formal SME registration translates to productivity improvement: the government's theory is that formalisation unlocks credit, training, and market access. Whether this holds in practice — or whether formal registration is just a compliance cost for small businesses — depends on the quality of the support ecosystem. 2. institution_vs_relationship.assessed_position: Vanndy's signature move is data systems: CamDX integration, SME data synchronisation across ministries, ISO membership, factory monitoring sensors. These are institutional infrastructure investments. His emphasis on 'informed decision-making and policy formulation' and his call for seamless data flows reflect a genuine orientation toward evidence-based governance. He is also Khmer Enterprise chair
subfields: Verdict: More institutional than average; data-oriented

global_positioning_logic.assessed_position: Vanndy's Singapore keynote pitch and ADB engagement reflect an 'alternative to China' positioning for mid-tier manufacturing. His emphasis on 30 SEZs across 12 provinces, the ISO membership, the ASEAN Plus Three science connections, and the Industry 4.0 framing are all designed to signal that Cambodia is moving up the value chain — not just a cheap labour destination. subfields: Theory: Position Cambodia as the innovation-friendly low-middle-income alternative to China
Overall: MEDIUM. Correct diagnosis; correct instruments; scale of implementation lags the ambition by a significant margin.
